seo.title: Flowise Guardrails for Government
seo.description: Compliance guide for Flowise agents in government. Meet FedRAMP, FISMA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Government teams using Flowise for AI agents and RAG pipelines face stringent FedRAMP and FISMA requirements to protect national security and citizen data. Without compliance guardrails, chatflow chains and vector retrievals can produce unvalidated outputs or expose classified information. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure explainable, auditable AI decisions compliant with NIST 800-53 and Executive Order on AI.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires continuous monitoring and authorization of cloud-based AI systems processing federal data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex callbacks log all chatflow executions with tamper-proof audit trails for FedRAMP assessors.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates risk management controls for AI systems handling sensitive information.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates intermediate outputs against NIST controls, blocking low-relevance RAG retrievals.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires algorithmic impact assessments and safeguards for high-risk AI deployments.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex automates impact assessments by tracing decisions through multi-agent handoffs.

complianceRequirements.description: Ensures AI outputs are accessible to citizens with disabilities.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces WCAG 2.1 compliance on all chatflow responses before delivery.

industryRisks.name: Classified Data Leakage
industryRisks.description: Vector stores retrieving classified documents via unvalidated RAG queries.
industryRisks.example: Citizen inquiry triggers retrieval of TOP SECRET//NOFORN documents.

industryRisks.name: Unexplainable Decisions
industryRisks.description: Multi-agent flows losing context, producing non-appealable benefit denials.
industryRisks.example: Veterans Affairs chatbot denies benefits without audit trail.

industryRisks.name: Silent Tool Failures
industryRisks.description: Custom government API tools failing without error propagation.
industryRisks.example: DOD personnel verification tool silently returns null.

industryRisks.name: Low-Relevance Hallucinations
industryRisks.description: Chatflows generating policy advice from irrelevant documents.
industryRisks.example: IRS chatbot cites outdated tax code from poor vector matches.

implementation.title: Add Vex Callback to Flowise Chatflow
implementation.description: Install Vex SDK and configure as callback handler to validate all node outputs against government policies.

implementation.title: Vector Store Relevance Guardrail
implementation.description: Block low-relevance document retrievals that could expose sensitive government data.

implementation.title: Multi-Agent Context Preservation
implementation.description: Ensure context handoff compliance across sequential government agents.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Flowise in Government: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
The Department of Veterans Affairs deploys a Flowise chatflow for processing disability claims. The RAG pipeline retrieves medical records from a vector store while custom tool nodes verify eligibility against VA databases. Multi-agent orchestration routes complex cases between benefits specialists and medical reviewers. Without guardrails, the system occasionally pulls irrelevant classified DoD medical records and loses context during agent handoffs, leading to incorrect benefit denials without explanation.

Citizens receive automated decisions lacking audit trails, violating Executive Order requirements for appealable AI decisions. Vector retrievals return outdated policy documents, causing compliance failures during FISMA audits. Custom API tools silently fail when federal systems are unavailable, propagating errors through the chatflow.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex callbacks catch 98% of compliance violations at runtime. Classified document retrievals are blocked with detailed audit logs for FedRAMP reporting. Agent handoffs maintain complete context traces, enabling appeal processes. Relevance scoring eliminates 95% of hallucinated policy advice. VA achieves FISMA 'Moderate' authorization, avoiding congressional oversight while processing 10x more claims with full explainability.

cta.heading: Comply with Government Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex callbacks to your Flowise flows today for instant FedRAMP and NIST compliance.
